title: he best indicator of ventricular preload?/

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: he best indicator of ventricular preload?/

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: The best indicator of ventricular preload is the end-diastolic volume (EDV) or end-diastolic pressure (EDP) of the ventricles. EDV is the volume of blood in the ventricles at the end of diastole (just before contraction), while EDP is the pressure within the ventricles at the same time. These measures give an estimate of the amount of blood in the ventricles and hence, the stretch or load experienced by the ventricular walls prior to contraction.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
Which has the highest risk of infection with blood transfusion ?
a. Cytomegalovirus
b. hepatitis B
c. hepatitis C
d. HIV

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: b. Hepatitis B has a higher risk of infection with blood transfusion compared to the other options listed. The risk of infection for each virus has significantly decreased over the years due to improved blood screening procedures. However, hepatitis B still carries a higher risk compared to cytomegalovirus, hepatitis C, and HIV.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
in which of the following do you give prophylactic antibiotics for endocarditis ?
a. Pacemaker
b. Coronary artery bypass (CABG)
c. Hypertrophic cardiomyopathy
d. Implanted defibrillators

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
a. Pacemaker

Prophylactic antibiotics are recommended for patients undergoing pacemaker insertion or other procedures involving the implantation of intracardiac devices, in order to reduce the risk of endocarditis.
